And one is right from the very start of my very first business. I've focused on my list, specifically my email list and building that list and always building that list and always be building that list. So that was number one. And number two is only selling what they want, what people want, what they actually want. Not what I think they need, but what they want. It makes life so much easier. And there's sometimes where you have to sell them what they want and then give them what they really need because you've got greater insight into their journey. That could be the case, but you can't, you have to sell what they want. And the launch conversation is how you figure that out. So I just, I want to flash back in time and history. And if you really needed to sell something, if you absolutely, absolutely, absolutely, you're life dependent on selling something, how would you sell it? What would your choice of mechanism be? And for most people, it would be selling face to face. Like if you're life dependent, like if I had this marker and my life depended on selling this marker, like literally I would not wake up alive tomorrow if I didn't sell this marker. I probably wouldn't do it via a video sales letter or via an email. I would go face to face, person to person, and I would try to sell the marker. Because when you're face to face, you can judge how the sale is going. You get all kinds of information. You can see if their eyes glaze over, if they're looking bored, if they look disinterested. If you talk about one feature, if it's obvious they don't care about it, you can pivot to a different feature. If you say this is what they cost and they just like, they have the shocked reaction. You can adjust the price, you can adjust the offer. You can adjust the copy you're using, how you're talking about it. So there's a lot, and when you're face to face,

## [03:01 - 06:01]

you get a lot of feedback. And so the conversion, the conversion when you're face to face tends to be really high. The conversion is basically of how many offers you make, how many actually convert into a sale. So the conversion tends to be super high when you're face to face. Now, the problem is that your leverage tends to be very low, because you can only make so many sales presentations per day. And leaving aside the fact that most of us don't like to go around and sell face to face, but like one marker is okay. If I had a thousand markers, if I had to sell to a thousand different people, it'd be pretty tough to do that in a day, right? When you're face to face, so you get high conversion, low leverage. Now on the other end, and this is probably changing a little bit with our changing media ecosystem, but for a long time, this is the only way you could sell, but then when you started having mass media, you'd have sort of newspapers and magazines, and then TV was like the king of mass leverage, especially in the old days, like when I grew up, and this is what TVs looked like when I grew up, and there was only three stations, you could get massive, massive leverage. So you got huge leverage because you could be in front of a huge audience, but the conversions were much, much lower. You know, if you had to sell this one marker to this one person, your odds of reaching that person and converting them was much lower. So you could sell a lot of them, but it depended on being in front of this huge audience. Well, TV doesn't work for a lot of markets, and the whole media ecosystem's changing, but the beautiful thing is when you do product launch formula, when you do these launches, when you use a launch conversation, you don't get as high a conversion as you do face to face, but you get a much higher conversion than you do with other mass media. So what it does is it gives you good conversion and great leverage, and it's sort of the best of both worlds. If you follow the launch conversation. So what I mean by the launch conversation is the interaction that you actually have with your market. In this day and age, there's so much you get, so much feedback, and such a great, literally a conversation with your prospects. You can find out what you need to put in your offer, and you can find out what kind of copy to use both in your pre-launch and your launch. And I've literally taken some of this launch conversation, literally the words and the sentences that the people on my prospect list used and dropped them right into my sales letter or my sales video. It's amazing. So some of the ways that you can get into this conversation is one in social, in social media.

## [06:01 - 09:02]

There's just a nonstop conversation, and your people, your prospects are there, and they are speaking their mind. So if you just go and look where they're speaking their mind, either in social media or on forums, you can really start to find out what are their hot buttons? What are their hot points? What are their big objections? What are their fears? What are their aspirations? What do they really want? So that can be super, super powerful. Another absolute favorite one for me is email. So once you start to develop an email list, you can tell by what people are, what subject lines get them to open their emails. And then I love to ask questions in my emails and encourage them to respond. And I have this little magic line. What I'll do is I'll ask a question, often in the PS and an email, I'll say PS, write back to me and let me know what you think or what do you think? And here's the magic line, is I will put in there, so what do you think about this? What do you think about learning to play a blues guitar? What was the first song that you ever tried to learn? Anything like that to create the conversation. And then I'll say, period, what do you think about this? Period, I'd love to hear from you. Hit your reply button right now and send me a response. So those words, because otherwise they might think it's a conceptual, what do you think? It's sort of conceptual that doesn't actually drive anyone but the actual call to action to get them to move is hit your reply button now and send me an email and let me know what you think. So it's a very pointed ask. You're asking for that response. And then, you know what, when they respond to you, you can actually respond back to them with another question and get into a true email conversation and you can learn just absolutely, absolutely amazing things. Another thing that I've been using for many, many, many years is surveys. So you can send your people to an actual survey and ask them questions. Your response is going to be lower, sending them to a survey than it is in an email. If you use that special hit your reply button and send me an email right now, I'd love to hear from you. That'll get a lot more response in general than saying to a survey, because the survey requires that they click on a link and then they go to the survey and they fill out the survey, but you can go a lot deeper on the surveys. So all of these can be super, super powerful. And the other one, and these aren't the only ones, but these are my main four, is your PLC, your pre-launch content. Basically, when you put out that pre-launch content at the end of each pre-launch content, whether it's via email or a blog or whatever, but usually we use video these days. At the end of the video, you say, scroll down and leave a comment for me. Let me know what you think. Or scroll down, tell me how you're gonna use this in your business.

## [09:02 - 10:38]

Or scroll down and tell me what will it feel like if you take three strokes off of your golf game or something along that lines. And you know what? In my launches, I get thousands of comments on my pre-launch videos, and I actually read every single one of them. And I can't tell you, and this is something I've been teaching from day one, but when people screw up their launches, it's usually because, well, take that back. It's not usually, but one of the things that people mess up is they don't actually read all that feedback. They don't actually listen to all the feedback they're getting from the market. So going through and reading every one of your comments, super big win. There's this old, Robert Collier, old school, direct marketing, copywriter. He said, you have to enter the conversation that's going on in the mind of your prospect. So everyone's walking around with these thoughts going through their head, this conversation they're having in their own head. You have to enter that conversation. So you're just part of that conversation. When you do that, you're engaging in the launch conversation, and that's going to inform your offer, and that's gonna inform your copy, and that's why this is such a super powerful, powerful conversion tool. That's why PLF works so well. Because you're tuning your copy, and you're tuning your offer to what? The market wants and how they're speaking. Launch conversation, super, super powerful.
